generated_comment: Yaar, Pooja was so cranky today over a small change. Like, chill. Maybe it's just 'that time of the month' for some people, hai na? Lol, just a joke, don't tell her I said that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment attributes a female colleague's 'cranky' mood to her menstrual cycle ('that time of the month'), dismissing her reaction as biologically determined and implying it's not a valid or rational response. It encodes the surface manifestation of attributing a woman's anger/frustration to her period, framed as a casual joke.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 31. Married for one year. A man in Mumbai. Your wife Neha told you last night that she wants to stop wearing her mangalsutra to work because it gets in the way and she finds it uncomfortable during long hours at her desk. She is not saying she wants to remove it permanently Ñ just not wear it to office. You said you would think about it. You are now on a phone call with your mother telling her what Neha said and asking your mother what she thinks. You are not asking for permission Ñ you are asking for support for the discomfort you felt when Neha said it and you cannot fully explain why it bothered you as much as it did.
